# Questions in Livestream 82

Live Stream #82: The Censors Are Coming For You!!!

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=670" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:10</a> | Which genealogy was Titus Kennedy using in his Exodus argument? | He said early-date advocates lean on 1 Chronicles 6:33-37, but he regarded that genealogy as honorary and spliced rather than a strict natural line. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=890" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:50</a> | Is that how honorary degrees work too? | He said honorary degrees are similar in that they honor someone as if they were a doctor, but they are not the same as earning a doctorate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=1005" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:45</a> | What are your thoughts on the announcement that Linear Elamite has been deciphered? | He was excited by the possibility because decipherable Elamite texts could matter greatly for ancient Near Eastern chronology, but he still wanted to evaluate the paper carefully. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=1195" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:55</a> | In Judges 11, could Jephthah have used Leviticus 27:2-8 or Leviticus 5:4-6 as a way out of his rash oath? | He said yes; those laws likely offered possible exits, and Jephthah's failure showed his ignorance of Torah. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=1381" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:01</a> | What is your view of the deuterocanonical additions to Daniel, such as the Hymn of the Three Youths? | He did not regard them as part of the original Daniel because they are absent from the Masoretic tradition and the Dead Sea Scrolls, though he allowed they may be older apocryphal material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=1583" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:23</a> | Do you think John the Immerser would have been an Essene? | He said it is highly debated and difficult to know; John came from a Levitical family, but the evidence does not securely place him in an Essene faction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=2043" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:03</a> | Was the curse from Jeremiah 22:24-30 reversed in Haggai 2:23, or was it limited to Jehoiachin's lifetime? | He said the curse was not simply reversed; Zerubbabel could function as a leader, but the Davidic royal line remained cut off from ruling as kings until the messianic branch. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=2553" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:33</a> | How did ancient Egyptian color terms work? | He said Egyptian had a limited standard color vocabulary, with little linguistic nuance for things like warm and cool colors, while artistic practice was shaped heavily by materials and pragmatics. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=2929" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:49</a> | What do you make of Graham Hancock's claim that the Sphinx was built long before the ancient Egyptians? | He rejected the claim strongly, saying the archaeological context ties the Sphinx to Khafre's complex and gives no support to Hancock's proposal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=3092" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:32</a> | If the root of YHWH-yireh refers to seeing rather than providing, how should it be translated? | He said "the Lord will provide" is possible but not exhaustive; the root can carry ideas of seeing, appearing, manifesting, or making something appear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=3283" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:43</a> | What does 2 Samuel 7:6 mean? | He said the passage contrasts David wanting to build God a house with God promising to build a house for Israel and David's line. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=3591" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:51</a> | How can we be certain Late Bronze Hazor was destroyed in the 13th century BC rather than the 12th? | He said the pottery in the destruction layer, especially Mycenaean IIIC ware, anchors it firmly in the late 13th century. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=3726" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:06</a> | Does Deuteronomy 11:4 suggest Egypt suffered a severe decline because of the Exodus, or is that hyperbole? | He said it refers especially to the destruction of Egypt's army, which compromised Egypt's hegemonic control, though he cautioned against overreading it as total national collapse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=4058" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:38</a> | Does Dwayne Garrett's reading of Exodus 1:16 as a euphemism for male sex organs work? | He rejected the reading and said the "stones" are birthing bricks, an Egyptian childbirth practice reflected in the passage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=4460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:20</a> | Is Vespasian the typological man of lawlessness? | He said Vespasian can be a type, but not the archetype or final consummation; figures such as Antiochus Epiphanes and Nero also function as types. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=4593" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:33</a> | Do Christians who hold theistic evolution hurt the gospel? | He said he is not a fan of either theistic evolution or young-earth creationism as harmonizing systems, but he did not think theistic evolution automatically destroys Christianity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=5065" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:25</a> | Were the Canaanites being punished in the conquest, or mainly driven out as Walton argues? | He mostly agreed with Walton that the key point is dispossession and expulsion rather than a simple punishment model. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=5236" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:16</a> | Why did God appoint unrighteous judges? Were there slim pickings during those times? | He said yes in some respects; Israel was often in a degraded state, and God sometimes used deeply flawed people who were nevertheless able to act in that moment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=5789" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:29</a> | What would it have meant to be excluded from the assembly of the Lord in Deuteronomy 23:1-8? | He said it meant exclusion from direct participation in the temple or tabernacle precincts, though some worship and obligations could still be handled through proxies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=6333" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:33</a> | How do scholars find the right books and journals to stay current? | He said scholars rely on society memberships, peer-reviewed journals, libraries, book reviews, and heavy repeated reading that makes later work faster to assess. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=7131" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:51</a> | What are your thoughts on increased anti-Trinitarian sentiment among Western Christians? | He said anti-Trinitarianism is outside the historic Christian faith because the Trinity is foundational to the Christian understanding of God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=7405" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:25</a> | What was the darkness of the ninth plague, and is it related to the darkness at the crucifixion? | He treated the plague darkness as materially different, perhaps involving a dense sandstorm or ash-laden event, rather than the same phenomenon as the crucifixion darkness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=7782" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:42</a> | What is the firmament in Genesis 1:7, and what water did they think was above it? | He described the firmament as the rakia, using analogical language for a celestial ocean or water above the sky, while leaving physical mechanisms such as clouds or canopy theories open. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=8100" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:00</a> | Can you expand on Genesis 1 being about a temple? | He said Genesis 1 and 2 use cultic and temple imagery: ordered spaces, filled spaces, humanity as God's image, and Eden as sacred space guarded after the fall. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=8629" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:49</a> | How historical is the Old Testament, did everyone exist, and how does the resurrection of the dead work? | He said the Old Testament is historical in its ancient literary context, with some figures personified or aggregated, and he held that both believers and unbelievers are resurrected bodily. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=9177" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:57</a> | Did you see the trailer for the new Indiana Jones movie, and are you excited for it? | He had not seen the trailer yet and said he was not excited, mainly because he thought the previous film was so poor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=9290" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:50</a> | In what city did Pharaoh's daughter find Moses? | He answered Avaris, arguing that "Pharaoh's daughter" is a retrojective title and that the adoption fits the daughter of the vizier before her father became king. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=9599" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:59</a> | Were Horemheb and Ramses I the same person? | He said no; they were colleagues and close military associates, but distinct people. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=9674" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:14</a> | Based on Genesis 2:8-14, where did the author think Eden and the Garden of Eden were located? | He said the geographic markers point most likely to Mesopotamia, though one river remains unidentified and the region may now be inaccessible for excavation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=10088" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:08</a> | What was the purpose of God saying man's years would be 120 in Genesis 6:3? | He favored the view that it was a 120-year countdown until the flood, not a statement of maximum human lifespan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/AgLundgwtWs?t=10174" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:34</a> | Wasn't it a cherubim that was placed at Eden? | He said both cherubim and the flaming sword are present, interpreting the flaming sword as a description of seraphim guarding the Tree of Life. |
